Based on the Wikipedia page as of November 2025, Liu Zige holds the long course world record in the women's 200m butterfly at 2:01.81 (set in 2009). The only active swimmer to come close recently is Summer McIntosh, who swam 2:01.99 at the World Championships in July 2025. 

The timespan between November 2025 and February 2026 is predominantly focused on short-course (25m) swimming, climaxing with the World Short Course Championships in December. Few, if any, major international long-course (50m) competitions where swimmers are fully tapered for world-record attempts take place during the winter months. Therefore, the chance of this long course world record being broken before February 2026 is minimal, although uncertainty naturally increases at longer horizons as swimmers begin partaking in early spring meets.
